skill.name: analyzing-response-quality
skill.description: Provides a structured framework for evaluating AI response quality across correctness, completeness, clarity, efficiency, security, and usability.
skill.summary: Evaluate AI responses with structured scoring, gap analysis, and actionable improvement recommendations.
skill.author: C0ntr0lledCha0s

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported many high-risk patterns, but review found they are scanner rule definitions, markdown templates, or illustrative examples. No evidence found of command execution, network access, secret exfiltration, prompt injection, or malicious intent. The remaining risk is low because the package includes local helper scripts that read user-supplied files and produce heuristic reports.

low_findings.title: Local Helper Scripts Read User-Supplied Files
low_findings.description: The bundled Python helpers accept file paths or stdin and read local content for analysis. This is expected for quality checking, but users should run them only on files they intend to review.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Direct file reads are present in each script main routine. The behavior is local and user-directed, with no evidence of exfiltration.

low_findings.title: Documentation Describes Existing Scripts As Planned
low_findings.description: SKILL.md says validation scripts are planned and not implemented, while the repository includes those scripts. This is not malicious, but it can mislead users about available automation.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The markdown explicitly labels scripts as not implemented, and the static file list includes matching script files. This is a content accuracy issue, not a security threat.

low_findings.title: Static Dangerous-Pattern Findings Are Scanner Self-References
low_findings.description: The eval, exec, pickle, shell pipe, weak hash, private key, chmod, and document.write hits occur inside regex rule definitions or sample quality reports. No evidence found that the skill executes these constructs.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The suspicious tokens are embedded in pattern lists, messages, or illustrative markdown examples. I found no call path that runs them as executable code.

low_findings.title: Markdown Code Fences Misclassified As Shell Backticks
low_findings.description: Many external command alerts in SKILL.md and script files are markdown code fences or regexes that parse code fences. They are not shell backtick execution.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The referenced backticks delimit markdown blocks or are regex text for extracting markdown blocks. They do not invoke a shell.

low_findings.title: Environment And Secret Findings Are Rule Text
low_findings.description: The environment and secret alerts come from checker logic that looks for variable names and suggests os.environ.get. No evidence found that the skill reads environment secrets.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The code inspects AST assignment names and emits advice. It does not call os.environ or transmit detected values.

content.value_statement: AI responses can miss requirements, contain errors, or lack clear next steps. This skill provides a repeatable review framework with scoring, gap analysis, and recommendations.
actual_capabilities: Maps the original user request to delivered response requirements.
Scores correctness, completeness, clarity, efficiency, security, and usability.
Identifies missing features, missing documentation, validation gaps, and edge cases.
Provides a reusable quality report template with prioritized recommendations.
Includes heuristic helper scripts for code quality, completeness, and security checks.
limitations: Automated scripts are heuristic and can produce false positives or missed issues.
The skill cannot verify external facts without access to trusted sources.
It does not prove code correctness without tests or execution results.
Security review is checklist-based and does not replace a full audit.

use_cases.title: Review a Completed Coding Task
use_cases.description: Check whether a generated implementation meets requirements, handles errors, and includes usable explanations.

use_cases.title: Improve Technical Answers
use_cases.description: Evaluate whether an explanation is accurate, complete, clear, and useful for the intended reader.

use_cases.title: Run Consistent AI Self-Review
use_cases.description: Apply the same quality dimensions before finalizing complex responses or multi-step work.

prompt_templates.title: Basic Response Check
prompt_templates.prompt: Evaluate this response for correctness, completeness, and clarity. List the most important improvements first.

prompt_templates.title: Requirement Coverage Review
prompt_templates.scenario: Compare a request with an answer
prompt_templates.prompt: Map each user requirement to the response. Mark what was addressed, missed, or only partially handled.

prompt_templates.title: Code Answer Quality Audit
prompt_templates.prompt: Analyze this coding response for bugs, missing edge cases, security risks, maintainability, and test gaps.

prompt_templates.title: Full Quality Report
prompt_templates.prompt: Produce a structured quality report with scores, evidence, issue severity, gaps, strengths, and prioritized next steps.

output_examples.input: A generated API implementation answer needs review before use.
output: Requirement coverage shows authentication and request validation were addressed.
Error handling and rate limiting were missing from the proposed implementation.
Recommended next step is to add failure-path tests before integration.

output_examples.input: A long technical explanation feels unclear to a beginner.
output: Clarity score is moderate because key terms are introduced without definitions.
Completeness is strong, but examples need simpler inputs and expected results.
Recommended revision is to add a short summary before advanced details.

output_examples.input: A final response for a multi-step task needs a self-review.
output: The response completed the main workflow but did not mention verification limits.
Security considerations were noted, but no residual risk was stated.
Add a concise verification section and identify any untested assumptions.

best_practices: Start by restating the user requirements before scoring the response.
Use evidence from the response when assigning each quality score.
Prioritize fixes by user impact, correctness risk, and implementation effort.
anti_patterns: Do not give generic praise without citing specific evidence.
Do not treat a checklist score as proof that code works.
Do not ignore the user context when judging response length or detail.

faq.question: What does this skill evaluate?
faq.answer: It evaluates AI responses for correctness, completeness, clarity, efficiency, security, and usability.

faq.question: Can it review code answers?
faq.answer: Yes. It includes code quality, error handling, security, maintainability, and test coverage checks.

faq.question: Does it automatically prove an answer is correct?
faq.answer: No. It provides structured review and flags risks, but tests and source checks may still be needed.

faq.question: Are the helper scripts required?
faq.answer: No. The main skill is a manual review framework. The scripts provide optional heuristic checks.

faq.question: Can it help with non-code explanations?
faq.answer: Yes. It can assess clarity, structure, completeness, audience fit, and missing context.

faq.question: Is this skill safe to publish?
faq.answer: The audit found low risk. The bundled scripts read local files only when a user provides those paths.
